economic_notes: First Nation has loans to KIIKO Pimataka Limited Partnership for an algae-based Astaxanthin processing facility in St. Francois Xavier. Loans in arrears since August 2016 totaling $3,619,497. Partnership had no development activity during the year. First Nation also operates gaming, gas/grocery, ATM/tobacco, VLT, restaurant, and golf course enterprises.
notes: Audit opinion is QUALIFIED due to inability to determine fair value of loans receivable from KIIKO Pimataka Limited Partnership ($3,619,497). Loans have been in arrears since August 2016 with no payments received. Interest accrual suspended effective April 1, 2019. Auditor report dated January 15, 2025 — nearly 3 years after fiscal year end, indicating significant reporting delay. Cash increased dramatically from $46,175 to $12,990,450 year-over-year. Net financial debt improved from $(23,059,174) to $(6,939,228). 13 consolidated entities including gaming, construction, gas/grocery, ATM/tobacco, VLT, and investment operations. Total government transfers calculated as ISC + CMHC + First Peoples Development + Province of Manitoba + Dept of Canadian Heritage + Frontier School Division = $51,527,284.
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
High ISC dependency (78.45%) — operations heavily reliant on federal transfers
